title: rosa eglanteria var. villosa
intro: Rosa eglanteria var. villosa, commonly known as sweetbriar or wood briar, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rosa eglanteria var. villosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Rosa eglanteria var. villosa include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
